annotations.label: Langlands program

anchor.snippet: the Langlands program is a set of conjectures about connections between number theory

annotations.note: Grepping Mathlib for 'Langlands' returns no hits; the program as a whole is not formalized.

annotations.label: Objects for Langlands conjectures

anchor.snippet: Objects for which Langlands conjectures can be stated

annotations.note: No reductive-group / automorphic-representation framework for stating Langlands conjectures exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Artin reciprocity law

anchor.snippet: The Artin reciprocity law applies to a Galois extension of an algebraic number field whose Galois group is abelian

annotations.note: Grep for 'Artin reciprocity' and 'ClassField' in Mathlib finds nothing; class field theory is not yet in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Artin L-functions

anchor.snippet: For non-abelian Galois groups and higher-dimensional representations of them, L -functions can be defined in a natural way

annotations.note: No 'ArtinL' or Artin-L-function declarations found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Automorphic cuspidal representations

anchor.snippet: Langlands then generalized these to automorphic cuspidal representations

annotations.note: Grep for 'automorphic' and 'cuspidal' yields no representation-theoretic definitions in Mathlib (cusp forms exist only as classical modular-form objects).

annotations.label: Langlands reciprocity conjecture

anchor.snippet: conjectured that every Artin L -function arising from a finite-dimensional representation of the Galois group of a number field is equal to one arising from an automorphic cuspidal representation

annotations.note: Neither side of the proposed identity is defined in Mathlib, so the conjecture cannot be stated.

annotations.label: Local parameterization of L-packets

anchor.snippet: Over local fields this is expected to give a parameterization of L -packets of admissible irreducible representations of a reductive group over the local field

annotations.note: No notions of L-packet, admissible representation, or reductive group are present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Functoriality conjecture

anchor.snippet: The functoriality conjecture states that a suitable homomorphism of L -groups is expected to give a correspondence between automorphic forms

annotations.note: L-groups and automorphic forms are absent from Mathlib, so functoriality is not formalized.

annotations.label: Langlands dual group and L-function

anchor.snippet: Langlands constructs the Langlands dual group

annotations.note: Grep for 'Langlands' / 'dual group' in this sense returns no Mathlib results.

annotations.label: Functional equation for L-functions

anchor.snippet: One of his conjectures states that these L -functions satisfy a certain functional equation generalizing those of other known L -functions

annotations.note: Mathlib has functional equations for Riemann zeta and Dirichlet/Hurwitz L-functions (e.g. in `Mathlib/NumberTheory/LSeries/RiemannZeta.lean`, `AbstractFuncEq.lean`), but not the general Langlands L-function functional equation.

annotations.label: Functoriality Principle

anchor.snippet: He then goes on to formulate a very general "Functoriality Principle"

annotations.note: No formalization of the Functoriality Principle exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geometric Langlands program

anchor.snippet: The geometric Langlands program, suggested by Gérard Laumon following ideas of Vladimir Drinfeld

annotations.note: No geometric Langlands content found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Proof of geometric Langlands conjecture (2024)

anchor.snippet: In 2024, a 9-person collaborative project led by Dennis Gaitsgory announced a proof of the (categorical, unramified) geometric Langlands conjecture

annotations.note: Grep for 'Gaitsgory' returns no Mathlib hits; the categorical geometric Langlands proof is not formalized.

annotations.label: Langlands correspondence for GL(1)

anchor.snippet: The Langlands correspondence for GL(1, K ) follows from (and is essentially equivalent to) class field theory

annotations.note: Class field theory itself is not in Mathlib, so the GL(1) Langlands correspondence is not either.

annotations.label: Langlands conjectures over archimedean local fields

anchor.snippet: Langlands proved the Langlands conjectures for groups over the archimedean local fields

annotations.note: No archimedean Langlands content found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lusztig's classification

anchor.snippet: Lusztig's classification of the irreducible representations of groups of Lie type over finite fields

annotations.note: Grep for 'Lusztig' and finite groups of Lie type yields no relevant Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Wiles' modularity theorem as Langlands instance

anchor.snippet: Andrew Wiles ' proof of modularity of semistable elliptic curves over rationals can be viewed as an instance of the Langlands reciprocity conjecture

annotations.note: Grep for 'Wiles' returns only an FLT statement file; the modularity theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lafforgue's theorem

anchor.snippet: In 1998, Laurent Lafforgue proved Lafforgue's theorem verifying the global Langlands correspondence for the general linear group

annotations.note: Grep for 'Lafforgue' returns no Mathlib hits.

annotations.label: V. Lafforgue's automorphic-to-Galois direction

anchor.snippet: In 2018, Vincent Lafforgue established one half of the global Langlands correspondence

annotations.note: No formalization of V. Lafforgue's result is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kutzko's local Langlands for GL(2)

anchor.snippet: Philip Kutzko

annotations.note: Grep for 'Kutzko' returns no Mathlib hits.

annotations.label: Laumon–Rapoport–Stuhler local Langlands

anchor.snippet: Gérard Laumon , Michael Rapoport , and Ulrich Stuhler proved the local Langlands correspondence for the general linear group

annotations.note: Local Langlands over function fields is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Harris–Taylor local Langlands

anchor.snippet: Michael Harris and Richard Taylor proved the local Langlands conjectures for the general linear group

annotations.note: No Harris–Taylor local Langlands content in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fargues–Scholze geometric reformulation

anchor.snippet: Laurent Fargues and Peter Scholze recast the local Langlands correspondence in geometric terms using the Fargues–Fontaine curve

annotations.note: Grep for 'Fargues' returns no Mathlib hits (Scholze references are unrelated, e.g. condensed/perfectoid).

annotations.label: Geometric Satake for Fargues–Fontaine curve

anchor.snippet: prove a version of the geometric Satake equivalence for the Fargues–Fontaine curve

annotations.note: Grep for 'Satake' returns no Mathlib hits.

annotations.label: Categorical local Langlands

anchor.snippet: Their work also formulates a conjectural categorical form of local Langlands

annotations.note: No categorical local Langlands formulation found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fundamental lemma

anchor.snippet: Ngô Bảo Châu proved the " fundamental lemma "

annotations.note: Grep for 'Ngo' / 'fundamental lemma' (in the Langlands sense) returns no Mathlib hits.
